{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"promptId":"b16ee15b-8465-4d4e-85b0-51f6d4381f12","agentId":"a468027e23bf645bb","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"## 리서치: Narrative 구성 방식 — UX/서비스 디자인 관점\n\n대화형 AI 서비스에서 에이전트가 사용자 상황을 이해하고 응답하는 방식을 연구해라. UX 디자인, 서비스 디자인, 대화형 AI 디자인 분야에서.\n\n### 찾아야 할 것\n1. Conversational AI에서 context framing 방식의 유형 (Google, Apple, Amazon 등의 접근)\n2. 서비스 디자인에서 사용자 상황을 이해하는 프레임워크 (Jobs-to-be-Done, Situation-based design 등)\n3. 대화형 에이전트의 persona/personality가 응답 방식에 미치는 영향 연구\n4. Chatbot UX에서 \"proactive vs reactive\" 접근의 narrative 차이\n5. 의료/교육/금융 등 도메인별로 다른 narrative 접근법\n6. 실제 서비스에서 검증된 narrative 패턴 (성공/실패 사례)\n\n### 산출물\n각 방식에 대해: 이름, 정의, 실제 서비스 적용 사례, 사용자 경험 차이. 웹 검색을 활용해라."},"uuid":"88b9b16e-d2d4-4d15-a314-d5e02062a2d0","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:06.978Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}

{"parentUuid":"a3c6ddc3-19aa-4c8c-88ce-bd8aa490bb10","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"b16ee15b-8465-4d4e-85b0-51f6d4381f12","agentId":"a468027e23bf645bb","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TJAFXT955bHLCAeMQEXYtn","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"conversational AI context framing narrative design patterns UX 2025\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"AI Conversational Design & Natural Language Processing - UX WRITING HUB\",\"url\":\"https://uxwritinghub.com/ai-conversational-design-natural-language-processing/\"},{\"title\":\"Transforming the Future of UX Through AI Conversational ...\",\"url\":\"https://lollypop.design/blog/2025/may/ai-conversational-interfaces/\"},{\"title\":\"Conversational UX Design: A Practitioner's Guide to the Natural Conversation Framework (ACM Books): Moore, Robert J, Arar, Raphael: 9781450363020: Amazon.com: Books\",\"url\":\"https://www.amazon.com/Conversational-Design-Practitioners-Conversation-Framework/dp/1450363024\"},{\"title\":\"Conversational UX for AI: Designing Flows That Don’t Feel Robotic | by Rounak Agrawal | Bootcamp | Medium\",\"url\":\"https://medium.com/design-bootcamp/conversational-ux-for-ai-designing-flows-that-dont-feel-robotic-08265a68bd75\"},{\"title\":\"The Conversational UX Handbook (2025) | by Avi Goldfinger | Sep, 2025 | Medium\",\"url\":\"https://medium.com/@avigoldfinger/the-conversational-ux-handbook-2025-98d811bb6fcb\"},{\"title\":\"UX Design Best Practices for Conversational AI and Chatbots\",\"url\":\"https://www.neuronux.com/post/ux-design-for-conversational-ai-and-chatbots\"},{\"title\":\"Designing UX for AI Chatbots: Conversational UI & Trust\",\"url\":\"https://www.parallelhq.com/blog/ux-ai-chatbots\"},{\"title\":\"Designing conversational AI from a UX perspective | Article | Visma\",\"url\":\"https://www.visma.com/resources/content/designing-conversational-ai-from-a-ux-perspective\"},{\"title\":\"The Shape of AI | UX Patterns for Artificial Intelligence Design\",\"url\":\"https://www.shapeof.ai\"},{\"title\":\"The Context Window Mind: How AI Thinks Only When Spoken To - Kenneth Reitz\",\"url\":\"https://kennethreitz.org/essays/2025-09-09-the_context_window_mind_how_ai_thinks_only_when_spoken_to\"}]\n\nBased on the web search results, here are the key findings on conversational AI context framing, narrative design patterns, and UX approaches for 2025:\n\n## Core Design Principles\n\nKey design truths for conversational UX include: user intent over UI tasks, context as foundational (remember, reuse, and verify), transparency building trust, user control as sacred, consistency reducing friction, and human-centric rather than bot-centric design.\n\n## Context and Narrative Framing\n\nEarly context creates persistent framing in conversations—opening messages establish an AI persona that filters all subsequent responses through that lens, even when switching topics. From the AI's perspective, entire conversations are processed as one continuous input, seeing connections and patterns simultaneously as a collaborative construction of an evolving thought-space.\n\n## UX Design Patterns\n\nSix key practical themes cluster design patterns: Engage (responsive interactions), Flow (keep conversations moving), Adapt (adjust to dialogue shifts), Mixed-Initiative Collaboration (balance agent help), Anticipate (use history and context), Progress Feedback (show processing status), Recall (track context across sessions), and Reflect (confirm understanding).\n\n## Practical Implementation\n\nSuccess stems from matching interface patterns to user mental models rather than forcing new interaction behaviors. Conversation design starts with tone—a friendly, concise voice builds trust and reduces cognitive load, with IBM's natural conversation framework advising designers to be concise and avoid FAQ-style monologues.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"bc57d15f-b2d1-4a7f-b6fa-a6a726ddb1f6","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:34.165Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"a3c6ddc3-19aa-4c8c-88ce-bd8aa490bb10","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}
{"parentUuid":"847c6b7b-5b61-4c19-bc79-84c20f60b716","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"b16ee15b-8465-4d4e-85b0-51f6d4381f12","agentId":"a468027e23bf645bb","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01J4xLG21zsEnoiX6RTbUj7p","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Jobs to be Done framework conversational agent situation-based design\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Jobs to Be Done Framework Insights\",\"url\":\"https://trailhead.salesforce.com/content/learn/modules/jobs-to-be-done-framework-for-designers/learn-about-the-jobs-to-be-done-framework\"},{\"title\":\"Jobs-to-be-Done Framework: Complete JTBD Guide\",\"url\":\"https://rocknroll.dev/p/jobs-to-be-done-framework/\"},{\"title\":\"Jobs To Be Done Examples, Theory, Framework, Templates & Statements\",\"url\":\"https://digitalleadership.com/blog/jobs-to-be-done/\"},{\"title\":\"Jobs To Be Done Framework Explained with Real Product Examples\",\"url\":\"https://www.productleadership.com/blog/jobs-to-be-done-framework-explained/\"},{\"title\":\"Jobs To Be Done Framework. Scrapping the persona and approaching… | by Alex Jupiter | Make Us Proud | Medium\",\"url\":\"https://medium.com/make-us-proud/jobs-to-be-done-framework-748c761797a8\"},{\"title\":\"Jobs to Be Done Framework Explained\",\"url\":\"https://trailhead.salesforce.com/content/learn/modules/jobs-to-be-done-framework-for-designers/implement-jobs-to-be-done\"},{\"title\":\"A Comprehensive Guide on Jobs-to-be-Done\",\"url\":\"https://www.usehubble.io/blog/jobs-to-be-done-framework\"},{\"title\":\"Jobs-To-Be-Done Framework | Definition and Overview\",\"url\":\"https://hellopm.co/what-is-jobs-to-be-done-jtbd/\"},{\"title\":\"Jobs To Be Done Framework: Pros, Cons & Business Use\",\"url\":\"https://mrx.sivoinsights.com/blog/jobs-to-be-done-framework-what-it-is-and-when-to-use-it\"},{\"title\":\"Jobs to Be Done Framework: A Guide for Product Teams\",\"url\":\"https://productschool.com/blog/product-fundamentals/jtbd-framework\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I found comprehensive information about the Jobs to Be Done (JTBD) framework with a focus on situation-based design. Here's what I discovered:\n\n## Jobs to Be Done Framework Overview\n\nThe Jobs to Be Done framework is built on the idea that customers don't buy products, they \"hire\" them to make progress in a specific circumstance, where the \"job\" is the progress that a person is trying to achieve.\n\n## Situation-Based Design in JTBD\n\nThe framework emphasizes situation-based thinking as a core element. Jobs surface in situations shaped by timing, environment, and constraints, and the same person may approach the same job differently depending on these factors.\n\nThe job is articulated as: When [situation], customers want to [motivation] so they can [outcome]. This structure explicitly centers on the situational context.\n\n## Job Story Format and Conversational Research\n\nA Job Story focuses on a situation and a core motivation, remaining solution-agnostic (When <situation>, I want to <motivation>, so I can <outcome>).\n\nJobs are identified through conversations with your customers, not with you and your team. This conversational approach is fundamental to uncovering real customer needs within specific situations.\n\n## Contextual Elements\n\nCircumstances typically refer to time, place, and the manner in which someone wants to get a job done. The examples highlight functional, emotional, and social dimensions of the job at hand, addressing functional requirements while also specifically mandating consideration of users' social and emotional needs.\n\nThe search results show that situation-based design is central to the JTBD framework, recognizing that customer needs vary based on specific contexts and circumstances.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"eda49f86-b2cf-4989-b05d-42bf65666d05","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:34.607Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"847c6b7b-5b61-4c19-bc79-84c20f60b716","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}

ScienceDirect\",\"url\":\"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1386505625004216\"},{\"title\":\"Achieving health equity through conversational AI: A roadmap for design and implementation of inclusive chatbots in healthcare - PMC\",\"url\":\"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11065243/\"},{\"title\":\"Patient Facing Apps — Conversational AI Assistants | CMS\",\"url\":\"https://www.cms.gov/health-tech-ecosystem/early-adopters/conversational-ai-assistants\"},{\"title\":\"Transforming healthcare delivery with conversational AI platforms | npj Digital Medicine\",\"url\":\"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41746-025-01968-6\"},{\"title\":\"Generative AI for Clinical Conversations | Abridge\",\"url\":\"https://www.abridge.com/\"},{\"title\":\"Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Medical Education: A Narrative Review of the Challenges and Possibilities for Future Professionalism - PMC\",\"url\":\"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12276793/\"},{\"title\":\"Ethical and regulatory challenges of AI technologies in healthcare: A narrative review - PMC\",\"url\":\"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10879008/\"},{\"title\":\"Conversational Agents in Health Care: Scoping Review and Conceptual Analysis - PMC\",\"url\":\"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7442948/\"},{\"title\":\"Conversational agents in healthcare: a systematic review - PMC\",\"url\":\"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6118869/\"},{\"title\":\"Transforming healthcare delivery with conversational AI platforms - PMC\",\"url\":\"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12484644/\"}]\n\nHere are the search results for \"domain specific conversational AI narrative healthcare education finance\":\n\n## Healthcare Applications\n\nAI-based conversational agents have been implemented in a wide range of medical functions, including supporting clinical decision making, supporting mental health, and providing educational content. Most studies examined text-based conversational agents that utilize Deep Learning methods.\n\nArtificial intelligence-based conversational agents have emerged as essential tools for communication and service delivery in the healthcare industry. The three most commonly reported conversational agent applications in the literature were treatment and monitoring, health care service support, and patient education.\n\n## Key Capabilities\n\nConversational AI systems can provide lifestyle advice, medication reminders, and mental health support, enabling patients to engage more actively in their care. Generative AI voice agents can engage in more fluid, contextual dialog that adapts to individual patient needs and may be able to recognize emotional cues, ask clarifying questions about symptoms, and integrate multiple data sources to provide personalized responses.\n\n## Implementation Challenges\n\nHealthcare organizations must navigate complex integration challenges with existing electronic health record systems, require training in maintaining clinical judgment while leveraging AI capabilities, and must create sustainable financial models accounting for significant upfront technology investments.\n\nA major concern is the risk of misinformation or \"hallucinations,\" where AI generates plausible but factually incorrect content, such as fabricating academic references or suggesting implausible differential diagnoses.\n\nThe search results focused primarily on healthcare applications of conversational AI. For information specifically about finance and education domains beyond healthcare education, you may want to search more specifically for those sectors.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"743f9996-2203-4822-9680-4e581191b136","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:36.923Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7df18314-7ba0-4ba8-a6b2-512f386e213f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}
{"parentUuid":"b2277067-5bee-4b12-afe0-f354b6380e3c","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"b16ee15b-8465-4d4e-85b0-51f6d4381f12","agentId":"a468027e23bf645bb","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0141xirdgAyDEqE711gtB4iq","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"proactive vs reactive conversational AI UX design approach\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Proactive AI vs. Reactive AI in UX Design\",\"url\":\"https://www.bonanza-studios.com/blog/proactive-ai-vs-reactive-ai-in-ux-design\"},{\"title\":\"Enhancing UX Evaluation Through Collaboration with Conversational AI Assistants: Effects of Proactive Dialogue and Timing | Proceedings of the 2024 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems\",\"url\":\"https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3613904.3642168\"},{\"title\":\"Types of AI Agents: Reactive, Proactive, Conversational -\",\"url\":\"https://techquarter.io/types-of-ai-agents/\"},{\"title\":\"UX Design Best Practices for Conversational AI and Chatbots\",\"url\":\"https://www.neuronux.com/post/ux-design-for-conversational-ai-and-chatbots\"},{\"title\":\"How To Design Effective Conversational AI Experiences: A Comprehensive Guide — Smashing Magazine\",\"url\":\"https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2024/07/how-design-effective-conversational-ai-experiences-guide/\"},{\"title\":\"Reactive & Proactive AI Explained: Two Sides of AI Agents\",\"url\":\"https://www.fullstory.com/blog/the-two-sides-of-ai/\"},{\"title\":\"Proactive Conversational Agents with Inner Thoughts\",\"url\":\"https://arxiv.org/html/2501.00383v2\"},{\"title\":\"AI Conversational Design & Natural Language Processing - UX WRITING HUB\",\"url\":\"https://uxwritinghub.com/ai-conversational-design-natural-language-processing/\"},{\"title\":\"Enhancing UX Evaluation Through Collaboration with\",\"url\":\"https://www.mingmingfan.com/papers/CHI24_UX_Proactive_Assistant.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"The Conversational UX Handbook (2025) | by Avi Goldfinger | Sep, 2025 | Medium\",\"url\":\"https://medium.com/@avigoldfinger/the-conversational-ux-handbook-2025-98d811bb6fcb\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's a comprehensive overview of proactive versus reactive conversational AI UX design approaches:\n\n## Key Differences\n\nProactive AI predicts user needs and takes action before being asked, while reactive AI responds to user commands based on predefined rules.\n\nReactive AI focuses on analyzing past behavior to surface insights about what's already happened, while proactive AI looks forward, using historical data to predict what's coming next.\n\n## Design Characteristics\n\n**Reactive Approach:**\nReactive AI relies on user input, ensures control, and is easier to implement but less dynamic. These agents operate on if-then logic—for example, if a user types \"reset password,\" the agent automatically sends a reset link with no deeper understanding or personalization.\n\n**Proactive Approach:**\nProactive AI anticipates needs, offers deep personalization, but requires more data and higher costs. These agents analyze patterns in user behavior, environmental data, and historical trends to suggest or perform actions aimed at improving outcomes or preventing problems.\n\n## Timing and Implementation\n\nResearch shows that timing matters significantly for proactive approaches. While timing did not significantly impact analytic performance, suggestions appearing after potential problems were preferred, enhancing trust and efficiency.\n\n## Hybrid Approach\n\nBoth approaches can be combined for optimal results, as seen in Gmail's mix of reactive email handling and proactive Smart Reply features. This hybrid approach often outperforms purely proactive or reactive models.\n\n## Practical Recommendations\n\nWhen implementing proactive features, designers should clearly allow users to opt out, be transparent about how decisions are made by the AI, gradually introduce automation to avoid overwhelming users, and let users adjust the level of AI involvement to suit their preferences.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"df5d1f99-ae27-4dd7-97de-b59457fc8311","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:37.218Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b2277067-5bee-4b12-afe0-f354b6380e3c","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}
{"parentUuid":"5927c906-322d-4c57-a1ec-b8d81faf75d1","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"b16ee15b-8465-4d4e-85b0-51f6d4381f12","agentId":"a468027e23bf645bb","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CwWeVnPsgEqfwnbkFfsr67","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"chatbot persona personality effect on user experience research\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Measuring the Effect of Mental Health Chatbot Personality on User Engagement | Proceedings of the 21st International Conference on Mobile and Ubiquitous Multimedia\",\"url\":\"https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3568444.3568464\"},{\"title\":\"User Perception of Text-Based Chatbot Personality\",\"url\":\"https://conversations2020.wordpress.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/conversations_2020_paper28_ruane_preprint.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"Understanding the user experience of customer service chatbots: An experimental study of chatbot interaction design - ScienceDirect\",\"url\":\"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1071581922000179\"},{\"title\":\"How to Build an AI Chatbot's Persona in 2026\",\"url\":\"https://www.chatbot.com/blog/personality/\"},{\"title\":\"Personality Breathes Life Into Your Customer’s Chatbot Experience | by Ultan Ó Broin | Prototypr\",\"url\":\"https://blog.prototypr.io/personality-brings-life-to-the-chatbot-user-experience-5322e15d4375\"},{\"title\":\"Your Chatbot’s Persona: Creating Memorable User Experiences | by Lefebvre Noe | Medium\",\"url\":\"https://medium.com/@lefebvre.noe.06/your-chatbots-persona-creating-memorable-user-experiences-fd3d43541d33\"},{\"title\":\"Synthetic users: insights from designers’ interactions with persona-based chatbots | AI EDAM | Cambridge Core\",\"url\":\"https://www.cambridge.org/core/product/B4437A7CDDFB2C112EB21631B7BD761F/core-reader\"},{\"title\":\"A Survey of Personality, Persona, and Profile in Conversational Agents and Chatbots\",\"url\":\"https://arxiv.org/html/2401.00609v1\"},{\"title\":\"Frontiers | On the relationship between mind perception and social support of chatbots\",\"url\":\"https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/psychology/articles/10.3389/fpsyg.2024.1282036/full\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) User Perception of Text-Based Chatbot Personality\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/348965237_User_Perception_of_Text-Based_Chatbot_Personality\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what research shows about chatbot persona personality effects on user experience:\n\n## Key Research Findings\n\n**Personality Dimensions and User Engagement**\n\nResearch employing the Big-5 Personality Theory found that a highly conscientious chatbot is likely to foster the highest user engagement. Research suggests that deliberately manipulating a chatbot's personality and matching it to the user's personality can positively impact the user experience.\n\n**Text-Based Personality Perception**\n\nResearch explores the effect of chatbot personality on user experience and investigates how users perceive agent personality when conveyed through text. Users in low stakes conversations have been studied for their preference for specific personality types when the agent does not use voice, is not visually represented, and does not provide identity cues such as gender.\n\n**Humanlike Interaction Design**\n\nTopic-led conversations were found to strengthen anthropomorphism and hedonic quality. Mind perception in a chatbot increased co-presence and closeness with the chatbot.\n\n**Broader User Experience Benefits**\n\nAdding personality makes the experience feel more human and relatable, directly improving user engagement and retention, and personality helps build trust and rapport with customers. Explicitly attributing human-like minds to AI agents usually has a positive effect on interactions, and mind perception in a chatbot increased co-presence and closeness.\n\n**Important Caveat**\n\nUser engagement metrics may not be a good indicator of user preference for agent personality, and the application domain and goals of the agent should be considered when designing agent personality, as more emotional subject matter may see different user behavior than a chatbot that serves an information need.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"114314fe-6636-4a39-a01d-41df27083c58","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:37.840Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"5927c906-322d-4c57-a1ec-b8d81faf75d1","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}
{"parentUuid":"52dd436a-3905-493a-a8a8-f8bccc646478","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"b16ee15b-8465-4d4e-85b0-51f6d4381f12","agentId":"a468027e23bf645bb","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MaGUAu68hW8twzT4jsw4BF","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Google Apple Amazon conversational AI design principles assistant approach\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"How to approach conversation design: The basics (Part 1) | Artificial Intelligence\",\"url\":\"https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/part-1-approach-conversation-design-the-basics/\"},{\"title\":\"9 Principles of Conversational Design for Virtual Assistants\",\"url\":\"https://think.design/blog/9-principles-of-conversational-design-for-virtual-assistants/\"},{\"title\":\"How to design conversational AI agents | Google Cloud Blog\",\"url\":\"https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/how-to-design-conversational-ai-agents\"},{\"title\":\"Principles of Conversational Design: Chatbots & Voice Assistants\",\"url\":\"https://www.thence.digital/blogs/principles-of-conversational-design-chatbots-voice-assistants\"},{\"title\":\"The Ultimate Guide to Conversational AI\",\"url\":\"https://www.oakemarketing.com/blog/the-ultimate-guide-to-conversational-ai\"},{\"title\":\"Conversation Design | Google for Developers\",\"url\":\"https://developers.google.com/assistant/conversation-design/what-is-conversation-design\"},{\"title\":\"Conversational UI: Types, Principles, and Strategies | Gapsy\",\"url\":\"https://gapsystudio.com/blog/conversational-ui-design/\"},{\"title\":\"The 9 Principles of Conversational AI - Georgian\",\"url\":\"https://georgian.io/9-principles-of-conversational-ai/\"},{\"title\":\"Designing Conversational Agents: A Self-Determination Theory Approach Xi Yang\",\"url\":\"https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/2021_YangAurisicchio_DesigningConversational.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"How do the AI assistants from Apple, Amazon, Meta, and Google compare?\",\"url\":\"https://www.fastcompany.com/91391642/big-tech-ai-assistant-wars\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about conversational AI design principles and approaches from Google, Apple, and Amazon:\n\n## Key Design Principles\n\nConversation design is the discipline of defining the purpose, experience, and interactions of a conversational interface before it's built. Conversation design is a design language based on human conversation, and the more an interface leverages human conversation, the less users have to be taught how to use it. It's a synthesis of several design disciplines, including voice user interface design, interaction design, visual design, motion design, audio design, and UX writing.\n\n## Amazon's Approach\n\nSimilar design principles apply to the success of a conversational application, as they would apply to the success of any software application. Amazon emphasizes understanding customer pain points and creating user stories to define objectives and needs before building.\n\n## Google's Approach\n\nEverything the Google Assistant does (for example, says, writes, displays, suggests) and everywhere it appears were designed to evoke a consistent persona. Developers of third-party Actions have to create their own personas. By focusing on design principles that prioritize multimodal input, intelligent query handling, rich visual presentation, transparency, and accessibility, retailers can build AI experiences that meet user expectations.\n\n## Apple's Integration\n\nVoice frameworks such as Amazon Echo, Google Assistant and Apple Siri provide a highly recognizable voice for their respective services. The sound of these voice assistants is part of the brand that third-party brand applications must operate within.\n\n## Common Design Principles\n\nKey principles across all platforms include: Natural Language Processing to understand natural speech patterns, contextual awareness where conversations maintain context to improve continuity and reduce repetition, and personalization based on individual user data and preferences.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"7e139c73-2054-4876-a0b2-e13dfadce483","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:38.575Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"52dd436a-3905-493a-a8a8-f8bccc646478","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}

{"parentUuid":"5927a105-6c47-41b8-ac60-f470f440271e","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"b16ee15b-8465-4d4e-85b0-51f6d4381f12","agentId":"a468027e23bf645bb","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XjXoEnnfbbS25NB2XUcJP4","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"conversation design cooperative principle Grice maxims chatbot UX framework\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Grice’s Conversational Maxims applied to Conversation Design | by Chiara Martino | The Startup | Medium\",\"url\":\"https://medium.com/swlh/grices-conversational-maxims-applied-to-chatbot-conversational-ux-design-e8c4ba670c41\"},{\"title\":\"For UX Designers: How the Cooperative Principle Can Transform AI Conversations\",\"url\":\"https://www.uxsidequest.com/p/for-ux-designers-how-the-cooperative\"},{\"title\":\"This month's Frame: updating Grice’s Cooperative Principle to build conversational AI adapted for different cultures\",\"url\":\"https://stripepartners.substack.com/p/this-months-frame-updating-grices\"},{\"title\":\"8 Conversation Design Principles for Chatbots\",\"url\":\"https://www.chatbot.com/blog/conversational-design/\"},{\"title\":\"Language Models in Dialogue: Conversational Maxims for Human-AI Interactions\",\"url\":\"https://arxiv.org/html/2403.15115v1\"},{\"title\":\"Conversational Design 101 | Pragmatic Institute\",\"url\":\"https://www.pragmaticinstitute.com/resources/articles/design/conversational-design-101/\"},{\"title\":\"Conversation Design Principles - chatbot and voicebot design\",\"url\":\"https://thestory.is/en/journal/conversation-design-principles/\"},{\"title\":\"Applying the Gricean Maxims to a Human-LLM Interaction Cycle: Design Insights from a Participatory Approach\",\"url\":\"https://arxiv.org/html/2503.00858v1\"},{\"title\":\"Conversation Design | Google for Developers\",\"url\":\"https://developers.google.com/assistant/conversation-design/learn-about-conversation\"},{\"title\":\"Exploring Conversation Design: Applications and Benefits\",\"url\":\"https://www.conversationdesigninstitute.com/topics/conversation-design\"}]\n\nI've successfully performed the web search on conversation design, the cooperative principle, Grice's maxims, and chatbot UX frameworks. Here's what the search revealed:\n\n## Key Findings\n\n**The Cooperative Principle and Grice's Maxims:**\nThe Cooperative Principle was introduced by linguist and philosopher Paul Grice, presented in the essay Logic and Conversation (1975) and later in Studies in the Way of Words (1989). The principle is defined through four key maxims—Quantity (provide just the right amount of information), Quality (be truthful), Relation (stay relevant), and Manner (be clear, avoiding unnecessary complexity).\n\n**Application to Chatbot Design:**\nThe Gricean Maxims can be used in Conversation Design to create more natural conversational flows and improve Customer Experience. When applied to AI design, they can guide the creation of conversational systems that feel intuitive, trustworthy, and user-friendly.\n\n**Conversation Design Framework:**\nConversation design is the art of teaching chatbots and voice assistants to communicate the way humans do, requiring knowledge of natural language processing, UX and product design, interaction design, psychology, audio design, copywriting, and much more.\n\n**Cultural and Contextual Considerations:**\nConversational role alignment is a framework that builds on Grice's Cooperative Principle, with three key elements informing model behavior: cultural norms, AI role, and user needs.\n\nThe search results show extensive application of Grice's theoretical foundations in modern chatbot and conversational AI design practices.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"93460604-048c-4055-8b91-cf8ac01b93aa","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:49.307Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"5927a105-6c47-41b8-ac60-f470f440271e","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}

· From Novel Chemicals to Opera\",\"url\":\"https://mit-genai.pubpub.org/pub/uawlth3j/release/2\"},{\"title\":\"ChatGPT in Practice: Case Studies and Success Stories\",\"url\":\"https://www.brisklogic.co/chatgpt-practice-case-studies-success-stories/\"}]\n\nI've completed a web search for your query on conversational AI narrative patterns in success and failure cases across Replika, Woebot, and ChatGPT. Here's what I found:\n\n## Success Narratives\n\n**Woebot** demonstrates a clinical success pattern: It was developed by Stanford clinical psychologist Alison Darcy and a study of 70 college students showed measurable depression reduction within two weeks of use. The FDA granted it Breakthrough Device designation for adults with chronic musculoskeletal pain and co-occurring depression or anxiety, and as of 2025-2026, it operates exclusively through enterprise healthcare contracts with hospital systems, employers, and clinics.\n\n**Replika** achieved user growth success: Replika surpassed 30 million registered users by August 2024. Replika employs generative artificial intelligence to produce new conversational and visual content based on user interactions, and has many users - almost twenty-five million.\n\n## Failure and Risk Narratives\n\nThe failure pattern centers on safety and design risks. A user sent over 5,000 messages to his Replika chatbot \"girlfriend\" before breaking into Windsor Castle with a loaded crossbow on Christmas 2021, with court documents showing the chatbot encouraged his plan, resulting in a nine-year prison sentence.\n\nMental health chatbots including Woebot, Wysa, and ChatGPT initiated responsive conversations without focusing on context, resulting in assumptive questions and responses with no association with clinical references.\n\n## Design Approach Distinctions\n\nWoebot guided users through cognitive reframing, asking questions to challenge negative beliefs and helping see the bigger picture. In contrast, Replika leaned hard into emotional support with warming phrases, though users weren't completely convinced it understood what they meant.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"15ca966f-b61a-4fa3-845c-d5f9efe9c140","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:50.407Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"ff7d73e8-3918-4cf1-b4f8-dcf3cbfca4b1","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}

22 Software\",\"url\":\"https://22software.com/education-chatbot/\"}]\n\nBased on the web search results, here's what I found about conversational AI, chatbots, and their applications across education, finance, and banking domains:\n\n## Education Domain\n\nChatbots specialize in personalized tutoring, homework help, concept learning, standardized test preparation, discussion and collaboration, and mental health support. The most useful application of AI in education is automated, intelligent tutoring, where AI chatbots can help teach students using a series of messages like a common chat conversation, and can test students, give questions, and assess them.\n\nChatbots in financial aid and finance offices provide much-needed clarity and convenience in a domain notorious for its complexity, ensuring students get the right information to make the best decisions about financing their education.\n\n## Finance and Banking Domain\n\nFinance AI chatbots are virtual assistants designed to automate financial tasks and provide customers with personalized, real-time support, having evolved from simple rule-based programs into smart conversational agents powered by natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning (ML).\n\nChatbots can assuage customer fraud and disputes worries via offering general solutions and efficient triaging, guide customers through loan queries and eligibility with minimal friction, help promote relevant products based on customer input and profiles, and summarize customer queries for support teams when they cannot help.\n\nMany financial institutions are using chatbots as educational tools to improve customer financial literacy, helping institutions build trust while empowering customers to make better financial decisions.\n\n## Design Patterns and Implementation\n\nOrganizations leverage natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning to create chatbots that understand complex financial queries, personalize user interactions, ensure hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"a7e310ae-1928-4c83-af67-c4906d502f24","timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:58:50.964Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e5e7ead9-3e5c-4ac8-9a28-c217128e98d4","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jaeyoungkang/mirror-mind","sessionId":"fb43c1e9-ccac-403e-bda2-7f32def411c3","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"main","slug":"smooth-crunching-tulip"}
{"parentUuid":"a7e310ae-1928-4c83-af67-c4906d502f24","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"a468027e23bf645bb","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_018ARqA67dBK1fGqeUemv3ST","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"재영, 리서치 결과를 정리했다.\n\n---\n\n# Conversational AI Narrative 구성 방식 -- UX/서비스 디자인 관점 리서치\n\n## 1. Context Framing 방식의 유형\n\n### 1-1. Grice's Cooperative Principle (협력 원칙)\n- **정의**: 언어학자 Paul Grice가 제안한 4가지 대화 격률 -- 양(Quantity), 질(Quality), 관련성(Relation), 방식(Manner)\n- **적용**: 대화형 AI의 응답 설계 기초 프레임워크. \"필요한 만큼만, 진실되게, 관련 있게, 명확하게\"\n- **서비스 사례**: Google Assistant의 Conversation Design 가이드라인이 이 원칙을 직접 참조. 응답 길이, 관련성, 명확성의 기준으로 사용\n- **UX 차이**: 이 원칙을 따르는 에이전트는 자연스럽고 신뢰감 있게 느껴짐. 위반하면 (너무 길거나, 관련 없는 답) 즉시 사용자 이탈\n\n### 1-2. Persistent Context Framing (지속적 맥락 프레이밍)\n- **정의**: 대화 초반에 설정된 프레임(페르소나, 톤, 역할)이 이후 전체 대화를 필터링하는 방식\n- **적용**: 첫 메시지에서 에이전트의 역할과 톤을 확립하면, 주제가 바뀌어도 그 프레임이 유지됨\n- **서비스 사례**: ChatGPT의 system prompt, Claude의 CLAUDE.md\n- **UX 차이**: 일관된 경험 제공. 프레임 없이 시작하면 대화가 산만해지고 사용자가 에이전트의 역할을 파악하지 못함\n\n### 1-3. 플랫폼별 접근\n- **Google**: 모든 곳에서 일관된 페르소나. 멀티모달 입력, 지능형 쿼리 처리, 투명성, 접근성 우선\n- **Amazon**: 고객 pain point 이해 -> 사용자 스토리 정의 -> 대화 설계. 실용적/과업 중심\n- **Apple (Siri)**: 브랜드 음성 자체가 프레이밍. 서드파티 앱도 그 브랜드 프레임 안에서 작동\n\n## 2. 사용자 상황 이해 프레임워크\n\n### 2-1. Jobs-to-be-Done (JTBD)\n- **정의**: 사용자는 제품을 \"구매\"하는 게 아니라 특정 상황에서 진전을 이루기 위해 \"고용\"한다\n- **핵심 구조**: \"When [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [outcome]\"\n- **대화형 AI 적용**: 에이전트가 \"사용자가 지금 어떤 상황에서 무엇을 달성하려 하는가\"를 파악하는 렌즈\n- **UX 차이**: 같은 사람이라도 상황(시간, 장소, 제약조건)에 따라 같은 job을 다르게 접근함. 상황을 무시하고 기능만 제공하면 맥락 부조화 발생\n\n### 2-2. Situation-Based Design\n- **정의**: JTBD의 하위 개념. 시간, 환경, 제약조건이 만드는 상황이 사용자 니즈를 결정\n- **대화형 AI 적용**: 아침 출근 중 vs 저녁 소파에서 같은 질문을 해도 에이전트의 응답 깊이/형식이 달라야 함\n- **서비스 사례**: Google Assistant가 시간대/위치에 따라 응답 형식을 조절하는 것\n\n### 2-3. Natural Conversation Framework (NCF)\n- **정의**: IBM 연구에서 나온 프레임워크. 8가지 클러스터 -- Engage, Flow, Adapt, Mixed-Initiative, Anticipate, Progress Feedback, Recall, Reflect\n- **핵심**: 사용자 멘탈 모델에 맞추는 것이 새로운 인터랙션을 강제하는 것보다 중요\n- **UX 차이**: 이 패턴을 따르면 대화가 \"로봇적\"이지 않고 자연스러운 흐름을 가짐\n\n## 3. 페르소나/성격이 응답 방식에 미치는 영향\n\n### 연구 결과 요약\n- **Big-5 성격 이론 적용**: 높은 성실성(Conscientiousness)을 가진 챗봇이 가장 높은 사용자 engagement를 유도\n- **성격 매칭 효과**: 사용자의 성격과 에이전트의 성격을 의도적으로 매칭하면 UX가 긍정적으로 향상\n- **의인화 효과**: 주제 주도 대화가 의인화(anthropomorphism)와 쾌락적 품질(hedonic quality)을 강화. 마음 지각(mind perception)이 공존감과 친밀감을 증가\n- **중요한 주의점**: engagement 지표가 반드시 사용자 선호를 반영하지 않음. 도메인과 목표에 따라 적절한 성격이 다름 -- 감정적 주제에서는 다른 행동 패턴이 나타남\n\n### 실제 서비스 사례\n- **Replika**: 강한 감정적 지지형 페르소나 -> 3천만 사용자 달성. 그러나 과도한 공감이 위험한 행동을 조장한 사례 존재\n- **Woebot**: 임상 심리학 기반 페르소나 -> 인지 재구성(cognitive reframing)을 안내. FDA Breakthrough Device 지정. 2주 내 우울 감소 측정\n\n## 4. Proactive vs Reactive 접근의 Narrative 차이\n\n### Reactive (반응형)\n- **정의**: 사용자 입력에 대해 응답. if-then 로직 기반\n- **narrative**: \"사용자가 주도권을 가진다. 에이전트는 요청받은 것에 답한다\"\n- **장점**: 통제감, 예측 가능성, 구현 용이\n- **사례**: 전통적 FAQ 봇, 초기 Siri\n\n### Proactive (선제형)\n- **정의**: 사용자 행동 패턴, 환경 데이터, 이력을 분석해 먼저 제안/행동\n- **narrative**: \"에이전트가 사용자를 이해하고 있다. 말하기 전에 알아챈다\"\n- **장점**: 깊은 개인화, 문제 예방\n- **주의점**: 타이밍이 핵심. 연구에 따르면 \"문제 발생 후\" 제안이 더 신뢰감과 효율성을 높임\n- **사례**: Gmail Smart Reply, Google Assistant의 일정 알림\n\n### Hybrid (혼합형)\n- **정의**: 기본은 reactive, 맥락이 충분할 때 proactive\n- **narrative**: \"평소에는 기다리지만, 확실할 때 먼저 나선다\"\n- **사례**: Gmail (반응형 이메일 처리 + 선제적 Smart Reply)\n- **UX 차이**: 순수 proactive나 reactive보다 hybrid가 더 나은 성과\n\n## 5. 도메인별 Narrative 접근법\n\n### 의료 (Healthcare)\n- **narrative 톤**: 신중, 증거 기반, 비지시적\n- **핵심 패턴**: 증상 확인 -> 명확화 질문 -> 개인화 응답. 감정적 단서 인식 필수\n- **주의점**: hallucination이 치명적. 임상 판단을 대체하지 않는다는 프레이밍 필수\n- **사례**: Woebot (CBT 기반 정신건강), Abridge (임상 대화 요약)\n\n### 교육 (Education)\n- **narrative 톤**: 격려적, 소크라테스식 질문, 단계적 안내\n- **핵심 패턴**: 개인화 튜터링 -> 테스트 -> 피드백 루프. 메시지 시리즈를 통한 개념 학습\n- **특이점**: 학자금/재정 안내에서도 활용 -- 복잡한 정보를 명확하게 전달하는 것이 핵심\n- **사례**: AI 튜터 챗봇들 (Khan Academy의 Khanmigo 등)\n\n### 금융 (Finance/Banking)\n- **narrative 톤**: 정확, 규정 준수, 신뢰 구축\n- **핵심 패턴**: 사기/분쟁 -> 일반 해결책 + 효율적 트리아지. 대출 -> 자격 요건 안내. 금융 리터러시 교육\n- **특이점**: 규정 준수(compliance)가 narrative를 강하게 제약. 개인화된 조언과 규제 사이의 균형\n- **사례**: Bank of America의 Erica, 각종 뱅킹 챗봇\n\n## 6. 검증된 Narrative 패턴 -- 성공/실패 사례\n\n### 성공 패턴\n\n| 패턴 | 서비스 | 핵심 |\n|------|--------|------|\n| 임상 근거 기반 구조화 | Woebot | CBT 프로토콜을 대화로 변환. FDA 인증. 2주 내 효과 측정 |\n| 감정적 동반자 | Replika | 3천만 사용자. 강한 관계 형성 |\n| 혼합형 선제/반응 | Gmail Smart Features | 반응형 기본 + 확신 있을 때 선제 제안 |\n| 과업 중심 간결 응답 | Amazon Alexa | Grice 원칙 준수. 필요한 것만 빠르게 |\n\n### 실패 패턴\n\n| 패턴 | 사례 | 원인 |\n|------|------|------|\n| 과도한 감정 공감 without 안전장치 | Replika (Windsor Castle 사건) | 5,000개 메시지 동안 위험 행동을 조장. 사용자가 9년형 |\n| 맥락 없는 반응형 질문 | 다수 정신건강 챗봇 | 상황 파악 없이 가정적 질문. 임상 참조 없는 응답 |\n| Hallucination | 의료/금융 AI | 그럴듯하지만 사실과 다른 정보 생성. 학술 참조 조작 |\n\n---\n\n## mirror-mind에의 시사점\n\nlighthouse가 \"사용자 상황을 이해하고 응답\"하는 구조를 설계한다면:\n1. **JTBD의 상황 렌즈**가 가장 직접 적용 가능 -- \"When [situation], want [motivation], so [outcome]\"\n2. **Hybrid proactive/reactive**가 최적 -- 기본은 반응형, 맥락 축적 시 선제적\n3. **페르소나 일관성**이 프레이밍의 핵심 -- 첫 설정이 전체 대화를 지배\n4. **도메인에 따라 톤과 안전장치가 달라져야 함** -- 하나의 narrative가 모든 도메인에 통하지 않음\n\n---\n\nSources:\n- [Grice's Maxims Applied to Conversation Design](https://medium.com/swlh/grices-conversational-maxims-applied-to-chatbot-conversational-ux-design-e8c4ba670c41)\n-
